title: silene italica subsp. nemoralis
intro: Silene italica subsp. nemoralis, also known as wood cranesbill or wood catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, Caryophyllaceae. It is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally employed to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive problems, and alleviating menstrual cramps, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, wood cranesbill is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be administered internally or applied topically to the affected area.
